description: Payment channel commands — create, fund, sign, verify, claim, close, and list XRPL payment channels

userStories.title: channel create command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to open a payment channel from the CLI so I can lock XRP for a streaming payment relationship.
acceptanceCriteria: Read PaymentChannelCreate docs at https://github.com/XRPLF/xrpl-dev-portal/blob/master/docs/references/protocol/transactions/types/paymentchannelcreate.md before implementing
Create src/commands/channel.ts and register it in src/commands/index.ts

xrpl channel create subcommand with: --to (required), --amount XRP decimal (required, converted to drops), --settle-delay seconds (required), --public-key hex (optional, derived from wallet.publicKey if omitted), --cancel-after ISO8601 (optional, converted to XRPL epoch: Math.floor(new Date(s).getTime()/1000)-946684800), --destination-tag n (optional)
Standard key material options: --seed, --mnemonic, --account/--keystore/--password
Standard output options: --node, --json, --dry-run, --no-wait
On success, output prints the channel ID (retrieved from transaction metadata CreatedNode with LedgerEntryType PaymentChannel)
tests/e2e/channel/channel.validation.test.ts covers: missing --to, missing --amount, missing --settle-delay, invalid ISO date, missing key material (no network)
tests/e2e/channel/channel.create.test.ts covers: create a channel and verify channel ID in output, --json, --dry-run
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: channel fund command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to add more XRP to an existing channel so I can extend its capacity without reopening it.
acceptanceCriteria: Read PaymentChannelFund docs at https://github.com/XRPLF/xrpl-dev-portal/blob/master/docs/references/protocol/transactions/types/paymentchannelfund.md before implementing
xrpl channel fund subcommand with: --channel hex (required, 64-char channel ID), --amount XRP decimal (required, converted to drops), --expiration ISO8601 (optional, converted to XRPL epoch)
Standard key material options: --seed, --mnemonic, --account/--keystore/--password
Standard output options: --node, --json, --dry-run, --no-wait
tests/e2e/channel/channel.fund.test.ts covers: fund an existing channel, verify updated amount in channel list output, --json, --dry-run
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: channel sign and channel verify commands
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to sign and verify off-chain payment claims so I can conduct micropayments without on-chain transactions.
acceptanceCriteria: xrpl channel sign subcommand with: --channel hex (required), --amount XRP decimal (required, converted to drops), key material options (--seed, --mnemonic, --account/--keystore/--password); no --node required; uses xrpl.js signPaymentChannelClaim(channelId, amountDrops, privateKey); outputs hex signature

xrpl channel verify subcommand with: --channel hex (required), --amount XRP decimal (required, converted to drops), --signature hex (required), --public-key hex (required); no --node required; uses xrpl.js verifyPaymentChannelClaim(channelId, amountDrops, signature, publicKey); outputs 'valid' or 'invalid'
tests/e2e/channel/channel.sign.test.ts covers: sign a claim and verify it passes channel verify, verify a tampered signature fails, verify wrong amount fails (no network required)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: channel claim command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to redeem an off-chain claim on-ledger or close a channel so funds are settled correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Read PaymentChannelClaim docs at https://github.com/XRPLF/xrpl-dev-portal/blob/master/docs/references/protocol/transactions/types/paymentchannelclaim.md before implementing

xrpl channel claim subcommand with: --channel hex (required), --amount XRP decimal (optional, converted to drops), --balance XRP decimal (optional, converted to drops), --signature hex (optional), --public-key hex (optional), --close flag (sets tfClose = 0x00020000), --renew flag (sets tfRenew = 0x00010000)
Standard key material options: --seed, --mnemonic, --account/--keystore/--password
Standard output options: --node, --json, --dry-run, --no-wait
CLI exits with error if --signature is provided without --public-key, --amount, and --balance

tests/e2e/channel/channel.claim.test.ts covers: destination redeems a signed claim (create channel, sign claim, redeem with channel claim), source closes a channel with --close flag, --json, --dry-run
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: channel list query command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to list all open payment channels for an account so I can see their IDs, balances, and expiry.
acceptanceCriteria: xrpl channel list <address> subcommand using account_channels RPC with optional --destination <address> filter

Default human-readable output per channel: channel ID, amount (XRP), balance (XRP), destination, settle-delay (seconds), expiration (ISO8601 or 'none'), cancel-after (ISO8601 or 'none'), public-key-hex
XRPL epoch timestamps converted to ISO8601: new Date((epoch + 946684800) * 1000).toISOString()
--json outputs raw JSON array
--node supported
tests/e2e/channel/channel.list.test.ts: create a channel then verify it appears in list; --json output; --destination filter returns only matching channels
Typecheck passes
